messages.message: Semantic thinness (hollow-significance): Catch lines that assert importance, sticking power, or consequence without naming a concrete referent or result. Matched template: {summarySubject} {linkingVerb} {weakJudgment}.
Dramatic colon found: "tools collect signals.". Rewrite without the short reveal after the colon.

Semantic thinness (deictic-summary): Catch deictic summary pronouncements that point at a supposed lesson, truth, promise, or part without naming the concrete claim. Matched template: {deicticSubject} {summaryVerb} the {evaluativeAdjective} {summaryNoun}.

Semantic thinness (hollow-significance): Catch lines that assert importance, sticking power, or consequence without naming a concrete referent or result. Matched template: {summarySubject} {feelingVerb} {weakJudgment}.

Semantic thinness (vague-threshold-change): Catch vague threshold or evaluation changes without named scale, cause, or concrete state. Matched template: {genericSubject} {thresholdVerb} {evaluativeComplement}.

Semantic thinness (hollow-significance): Catch lines that assert importance, sticking power, or consequence without naming a concrete referent or result. Matched template: {summarySubject} {linkingVerb} {importanceComplement}.

Semantic thinness (hollow-significance): Catch lines that assert importance, sticking power, or consequence without naming a concrete referent or result. Matched template: the {thinModifier} {summaryNoun} {linkingVerb} {weakJudgment}.

Semantic thinness (hollow-significance): Catch lines that assert importance, sticking power, or consequence without naming a concrete referent or result. Matched template: the {thinModifier} {summaryNoun} {linkingVerb} {weakJudgment}.
Generic signposting found: the-is-boring. Replace the frame with the concrete claim.

Semantic thinness (generic-lesson-extraction): Catch generic lesson, answer, fix, or point lines whose complement is a thin evaluative word or abstract noun. Matched template: The {summaryNoun} {linkingVerb} {thinComplement}.
Lesson framing found: the-strategy-is-boring. Replace the wrapper with the lesson.

Semantic thinness (solution-boring-frame): Catch solution-like nouns framed as boring, a consultant or LLM wrapper that praises unglamorous obviousness without adding substance. Matched template: the {solutionNoun} {linkingVerb} boring.
Generic signposting found: the-is-boring. Replace the frame with the concrete claim.

Semantic thinness (solution-boring-frame): Catch solution-like nouns framed as boring, a consultant or LLM wrapper that praises unglamorous obviousness without adding substance. Matched template: the {solutionQualifier} {solutionNoun} {linkingVerb} boring.

Semantic thinness (solution-boring-frame): Catch solution-like nouns framed as boring, a consultant or LLM wrapper that praises unglamorous obviousness without adding substance. Matched template: the {solutionQualifier} {solutionNoun} {linkingVerb} boring.

Semantic thinness (solution-boring-frame): Catch solution-like nouns framed as boring, a consultant or LLM wrapper that praises unglamorous obviousness without adding substance. Matched template: this {solutionNoun} {linkingVerb} boring.

Semantic thinness (vague-summary-cost): Catch vague all-of-that cost claims that summarize several actions as expensive or wasteful without naming the concrete waste. Matched template: {summarySubject} gets {costAdjective} when {ignoranceSubject} {knowledgeVerb} which {problemNoun} they are {workVerb}.

Semantic thinness (deictic-summary): Catch deictic summary pronouncements that point at a supposed lesson, truth, promise, or part without naming the concrete claim. Matched template: {deicticSubject} {changeVerb} the {changeObject}.

Semantic thinness (missing-conversation-plan): Catch content and strategy plans described through missing-conversation metaphors instead of a concrete missing page, query, or decision. Matched template: the {planNoun} is missing the {abstractObject} after the {triggerNoun}.

Semantic thinness (deictic-summary): Catch deictic summary pronouncements that point at a supposed lesson, truth, promise, or part without naming the concrete claim. Matched template: {deicticSubject} fits the {abstractSituation}.

Semantic thinness (different-job-summary): Catch short deictic lines that label a distinction as a different job, problem, question, or conversation without naming the concrete operational difference. Matched template: {deicticSubject} {linkingVerb} a different {genericWorkNoun}.

Semantic thinness (hollow-significance): Catch lines that assert importance, sticking power, or consequence without naming a concrete referent or result. Matched template: {abstractSubject} still has work to do.

Semantic thinness (hollow-significance): Catch lines that assert importance, sticking power, or consequence without naming a concrete referent or result. Matched template: {abstractSubject} now has {abstractPlural}, and the {abstractPlural} can {weakVerb}.

Semantic thinness (deictic-summary): Catch deictic summary pronouncements that point at a supposed lesson, truth, promise, or part without naming the concrete claim. Matched template: {deicticSubject} means the {artifactNoun} has to contain {abstractObject} worth {abstractGerund}.
Generic signposting found: that-is-useless. Replace the frame with the concrete claim.

Generic signposting found: that-is-useless. Replace the frame with the concrete claim.

Semantic thinness (deictic-summary): Catch deictic summary pronouncements that point at a supposed lesson, truth, promise, or part without naming the concrete claim. Matched template: {deicticSubject} {meaningVerb} {abstractSubject} {linkingVerb} {weakJudgment}.
Negation reframe found: "That does not mean engagement is useless. It means the post has to contain source material worth retrieving.". Rewrite without the not-X-then-Y construction.

Semantic thinness (abstract-metaphor-claim): Catch slogan-like abstract metaphors where an abstract subject performs a physical or spatial action with no concrete referent. Matched template: {artifactSubject} {metaphorVerb} its {answerNoun} to {abstractDestination}.

Semantic thinness (abstract-personification-line): Catch abstract nouns treated as social actors in short payoff lines without concrete action or cause. Matched template: {abstractSubject} entered {socialPlace}.
